rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Aw Ard As Judgment; Memorandum Of Law
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 30, 2025, Line 1. PETITIONER BROADCAST MUSIC INC. Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Aw Ard As Judgment. Petitioner Broadway Music Inc.'s unopposed Petition to Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award as Judgment is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the October 25, 2024, contractual arbitration award is confirmed as a judgment in favor of Broadcast Music Inc., as petitioner, and against Zen Compound LLC dba Temple Nightclub, as respondent, in the principal sum of $22,156.00, together with prejudgment interest of $909.41 (based on the daily rate of $4.31 per day from October 1, 2024, through April 30, 2025, at the contract rate of 9% per annum), plus recoverable costs of suit of $555.00, for a total judgment of $23,620.41. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ARCHITECTURAL METAL INC. VS. THOMPSON BUILDERS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Person[S] Most Qualified Of Thompson Builders Corporation To Appear And Testify At Deposition And To Produce Documents; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Thompson Builders Corporation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 30, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF ARCHITECTURAL METAL INC.'s Motion For Order Compelling Person[S] Most Qualified Of Thompson Builders Corporation To Appear And Testify At Deposition And To Produce Documents; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Thompson Builders Corporation. Plaintiff Architectural Metal Inc.'s Motion For Order Compelling Person[s] Most Qualified Of Thompson Builders Corporation To Appear And Testify At Deposition And To Produce Documents; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Thompson Builders Corporation is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff has demonstrated reasonable meet and confer for the discovery at issue. Plaintiff's PMK deposition notice and related RFP are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant Thompson Builders Corporation has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. The overarching objection regarding the scope of proceedings and trial is overruled, consistent with the court's April 29, 2025, order. To be clear, the court finds Plaintiff's discovery is not barred by the 2021 Agreement. The court finds that the standard for discovery is not now "necessary." The PMK deposition(s) and the document production must commence no later than May 7, 2025, and be completed no later than May 13, 2025. As to any documents withheld on claim of privilege, Defendant shall produce a Code-compliant privilege log at the time of the production is commenced under this order. The court does not find Defendant acted with substantial justification. Defendant shall pay $3,425 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 20 court days from notice of this order. To the extent Plaintiff seeks sanctions in excess of this sum, the request is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY LEE, ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, VS. PANDA EXPRESS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Second Renewed Motion And Second Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 30, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY LEE's Motion And Second Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action. Plaintiff Jeffrey Lee's unopposed Second Renewed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court orders the case dismissed as follows: (1) Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims alleged to have occurred from April 14, 2021, to December 16, 2024, (which is time period included in the Minas Settlement) are dismissed with prejudice; (3) Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims alleged that fall outside of the Minas PAGA Period are dismissed without prejudice; and, (3) Plaintiff's individual PAGA claims in full are dismissed with prejudice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
